title: stevia yaconensis var. subeglandulosa
intro: Stevia yaconensis var. subeglandulosa, also known as the yacon stevia or sweet yacon, is a perennial plant that is a variant of the Stevia rebaudiana plant. This plant is native to South America and is known for its sweet and slightly earthy flavor, making it a popular natural sweetener. The medicinal properties of Stevia yaconensis var. subeglandulosa include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming Stevia yaconensis var. subeglandulosa include weight loss, improved digestion, and reduced risk of diabetes and cardiovascular disease, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or sweetener by extracting its leaves and roots.
